game_name: Might and Magic VI: The Mandate of Heaven

element_introduction_order.mastery_context: navigating scalable mountains and open world
using barrels, cauldrons, shrines, wells, fountains throughout game
affects availability of timed events and encounters
two tiers of promotion per class available

typical_relief_triggers: returning to settlements for level training
finding equipment upgrades and stat-boosting items
typical_tension_triggers: random enemy encounters in overland exploration
time-sensitive scheduled events (Solstices, Circus of the Sun)
enemy difficulty scaling with party progression
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Player-driven exploration rhythm with optional side content allowing flexible tension management; calendar system creates periodic narrative pressure points

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Non-linear map design allows optional exploration; scalable mountains and overland features suggest elevated areas contain optional content
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Item containers (barrels, cauldrons, wells, fountains) scattered throughout world suggest systematic exploration rewards hidden stat boosts

difficulty_curve.early_game: Low starting attributes require progression through accessible quests and enemy encounters; skill acquisition begins immediately
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Non-linear exploration opens multiple difficulty paths; promotion quests provide structured difficulty tiers; player choice determines pacing
difficulty_curve.late_game: Two-tier promotion system creates end-game challenge ceiling; class-specific skill mastery and stat optimization become critical
notable_spikes: Transition from early settlement-based play to open overland exploration

notable_level_patterns.description: Towns and settlements serve as safe zones for training, guild skill acquisition, and quest pickup; radiating outward leads to increasing danger
Mountains and elevation changes in overland exploration create optional difficulty zones; higher elevations correlate with increased challenge
Interior exploration uses first-person perspective with full 360-degree freedom; distinct from overland third-person exploration
Specific events (Solstices, Circus of the Sun) tied to in-game calendar create temporal pacing distinct from spatial progression
Each of six classes has unique promotion quest chains providing structured progression gates within non-linear exploration
